message:
refactor(workflows/runner): deep cx threading through abort_execution_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Tenth caller-migration tick in the strangler-fig phase.

`WorkflowRunner::abort_execution_with_cx` upgraded from pre-flight
delegate to fully cx-threaded body. Routes both internal storage
calls through cx-first siblings:

- storage.get_workflow → get_workflow_with_cx
- storage.upsert_workflow → upsert_workflow_with_cx

Plus a cx.checkpoint() seam between the two storage calls so a
late-cancelled caller can bail after reading the record but before
persisting the aborted state mutation.

Legacy `abort_execution` preserved unchanged.

Scope boundary: `handle_detection_with_cx` is also a pre-flight
delegate but its underlying `handle_detection` + `run_workflow`
stack has 10+ storage call-sites across hundreds of LOC. Migrating
that chain is out of scope for this tick — deferred as a larger
follow-up.

Verified via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/
ft-rusticmaple-target, --features asupersync-runtime): 31/31
workflows::runner tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
